title: Joe and Michael Reconnect
summary: Joe Furnari advised Michael Donovan to approach website development in two phases: a quick refresh for Vinnie's immediate needs, followed by a strategic buildout. Michael expressed significant frustration over the extensive workload, feeling it's beyond his VP of Marketing role, and highlighted the existing website's complexity. Joe suggested focusing on five core page examples for Vinnie by Friday to secure quick wins and build confidence for future strategic work, acknowledging resource limitations.

decisions.description: Website development will proceed in two phases: quick refresh then strategic buildout.
Michael will create five core page examples for Vinnie by Friday.

actionItems.task: Respond to Vinnie's email regarding his comments.
Create five new website pages (or 1-2) and send examples/ideas.
Position a call with Vinnie to review the new pages.

tensions: Michael's frustration with extensive workload and acting as a one-person marketing team.
Michael feeling current work is outside his VP of Marketing responsibilities and questioning demotion.
Michael's frustration with the existing website's 'bloated' complexity (43 plugins, 5 themes).
Michael's concern about Vinnie using ChatGPT out of context from his email.
Joe acknowledging limited resources and duplicated effort as a short-term issue.
Michael feeling 's*** on and attacked' and lack of team support.
Michael's concern about 'AI delusion' affecting business decisions.
referencesToPast: Conversation with Joe in March or April about Michael doing too much.
Offsite meeting where Vinnie 'bit off' Joe's head.
Michael building websites for Strolid and Stral AI without compensation.
Michael's conversation with Vim before starting.
Marketing plan document reviewed last week.
Joe's conversation last week about duplication of effort.

Hiring Z and subsequent conversations with Natalie and Joe.

Advised delivering short-term requirements to gain flexibility for long-term work.
Noted Vinnie's frustration with meetings and preference for simple visuals.
